steps.prompt: Which word is a noun meaning 'time'?

hints.rerum: 'rerum' is a noun but means 'of things', not 'time'
hints.vetustas: 'vetustas' is a noun meaning 'old age/antiquity', not 'time'

hints.Accusative: 'Tempus' can be accusative, but here it is the subject of an apostrophe — nominative

hints.2nd: 2nd declension neuter nouns end in -um — 'tempus' with genitive 'temporis' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns have genitive -us — 'tempus' has genitive 'temporis'
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'tempus' is a 3rd declension neuter noun

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'res' is 'res' (singular) or 'res' (plural) — 'rerum' with -um ending is genitive plural
hints.Accusative: Accusative plural of 'res' would be 'res' — 'rerum' is genitive plural

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'res' with genitive 'rei' is 5th declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension has varied nominatives — 'res' with genitive 'rei' is distinctly 5th declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'res' is 5th declension

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'vetustas' with genitive 'vetustatis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'vetustas' is a 3rd declension -tas noun
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'vetustas' with its -tatis genitive is 3rd declension

hints.rerum: 'rerum' is genitive plural — 'edax' is nominative and agrees with 'Tempus'
hints.vetustas: 'vetustas' has its own modifier 'invidiosa' — 'edax' agrees with 'Tempus'

hints.but: 'but' would be 'sed' or 'autem' — '-que' is an enclitic conjunction meaning 'and'
hints.not: 'not' would be 'non' or 'ne' — '-que' is an enclitic meaning 'and'

translation: O Time, devourer of things, and you, envious old age
